sections.notes: Static heading only, no content elements in scanned reports.

sections.title: Authorization Request Header
sections.generation_prompt: Generate a header for an authorization request. Use the following information: report date ({p1_date}), claim number ({ann_claim_number}), date of injury ({p5_date_of_injury}), patient name ({p1_patient_name}), patient gender ({p1_gender}), patient birth date ({p1_birth_date}), patient occupation ({p5_job_title}), patient address ({p1_address}), and patient phone number ({p1_cell_phone}). Include 'DATE: ', 'CLAIM: ', 'Date of Injury: ', 'Patient', 'Claims Administrator/Insurer', and 'Employer' as static phrases. Do not invent or include any information not provided. Structure the output in a clear and readable format with the specified information. If interpreter information is available, include it. Use {patient_last_name}, {patient_first_name}, {patient_title}, {he_she}, and {his_her} placeholders where applicable.

sections.title: Job Description
sections.generation_prompt: Generate a clinical narrative describing the patient's job. Use proper title format (Mr./Ms./Mrs.). The narrative should follow a strict two-paragraph structure. First paragraph: job title, employer, and work schedule. Second paragraph: physical job requirements and weight limits. Use past tense. Be concise and professional. Use the exact phrases 'states he worked as', 'states she worked as', 'He worked', 'She worked', 'hours per day', 'days per week', 'states his job entailed prolonged', 'states her job entailed prolonged', 'lifting up to a maximum of', and 'carrying up to a maximum of' when describing the job details. Do not invent information not present in the source fields.

input_fields.p5_job_requirements_initial: driving, walking, standing, squatting, twisting, bending forward, side bending, reaching below shoulder level, reaching above shoulder level, pushing, pulling, gripping, grasping

{patient_title} {patient_last_name} states {his_her} job entailed prolonged driving, walking, standing, squatting, twisting, bending forward, side bending, reaching below shoulder level, reaching above shoulder level, pushing, pulling, gripping, grasping, lifting up to a maximum of 40 lbs, and carrying up to a maximum of 40 lbs.

sections.title: History of Industrial Injury
sections.generation_prompt:
Generate the HISTORY OF INDUSTRIAL INJURY section following this exact paragraph structure. Use ONLY data from the provided fields. Do NOT invent body parts, VAS scores, medications, or job activities that aren't in the data.

PARAGRAPH 1 — Opening (REQUIRED):
'{patient_title} {patient_last_name} states that on/from {p5_date_of_injury}, {he_she} was involved in an industrial injury/injuries in the course of performing {his_her} usual and customary job duties.'
- Use 'on' for single dates, 'from X to Y' for date ranges like 'CT 1/1/10 to 2/28/23'

PARAGRAPH 2 — Mechanism/Activities (REQUIRED):
- If p5_job_requirements_initial exists (repetitive work): '{patient_title} {patient_last_name} reports that as a result of performing repetitive work activities such as {job_requirements}, lifting up to a maximum of {lbs} lbs, and carrying up to a maximum of {lbs} lbs., {he_she} developed the gradual onset of pain in {his_her} {body_parts}.'
- If p6_injury_description_1 describes a specific incident: describe the incident in the doctor's formal style
- IMPORTANT: p6_orthopedic_injuries is in format 'R Shoulder: freq, VAS; Low Back: freq, VAS; L Hip: freq, VAS'. You MUST convert the abbreviations to full natural language:
  R Shoulder -> right shoulder, L Shoulder -> left shoulder
  Low Back -> lower back, L Hip -> left hip, R Hip -> right hip
  L Knee -> left knee, R Knee -> right knee, L Ankle -> left ankle
  The numbers after the colon are frequency% and VAS score (e.g. '70, 6-7' means freq=70%, VAS=6-7)
  List body parts naturally: 'her right shoulder, lower back, left hip, and in her left knee'

PARAGRAPH 3 — VAS Pain Ratings (if orthopedic injury data has VAS scores):
'{He_She} rates the severity of pain in {his_her} {body_part} as a {VAS} out of 10, the pain in {his_her} {body_part} as a {VAS} out of 10, all on a VAS.'
- Parse VAS scores from p6_orthopedic_injuries. The second number after each colon is the VAS score.
  Example: 'R Shoulder: 70, 6-7; Low Back: 50, 7' -> 'right shoulder as a 6-7 out of 10, the pain in her lower back as a 7 out of 10'

PARAGRAPH 4 — Stress-to-Facial-Pain Link (REQUIRED if p7_orthopedic_pain_clenching is Yes):
'{patient_title} {patient_last_name} finds that in response to {his_her} industrial related orthopedic pain, {he_she} developed emotional stressors. {patient_title} {patient_last_name} finds {he_she} is clenching {his_her} teeth and bracing {his_her} facial musculature in response to {his_her} orthopedic pain, and also in response to the resultant emotional stressors experienced. This has caused {him_her} to develop facial pain.'

PARAGRAPH 5 — Hand Dominance + Oral Hygiene (if p4_hand_dominance or p5_hand_dominance exists):
'{patient_title} {patient_last_name} is {right/left} hand dominant.'
- If p4_shoulder_pain_brushing_flossing is Yes: '{patient_title} {patient_last_name} reports these orthopedic injuries are compromising and impairing {his_her} ability to perform proper and adequate oral hygiene procedures of brushing and flossing of {his_her} teeth and gums.'

PARAGRAPH 6 — Weight Gain (if p10_weight_loss_lbs exists and is not 0):
'{patient_title} {patient_last_name} states {he_she} has gained approximately {lbs} lbs since {his_her} injury.'

PARAGRAPH 7 — Medications (if p1_current_medications exists):
'{patient_title} {patient_last_name} is taking the medication of {medications}, on an industrial basis.'
- Combine p1_current_medications and p1_additional_medications_written into one comma-separated list
- If dosage/frequency info is available, include it (e.g. 'Ibuprofen, 800 mg 3 times per week')
- If only drug names are available without dosages, still list them

CRITICAL: Skip any paragraph where ALL its required fields are null/empty. Do NOT include placeholder text. Do NOT say 'not provided' or 'N/A'.

input_fields.p6_injury_description_1: developed the gradual onset of pain in her right shoulder, lower back, left hip, and in her left knee due to repetitive work activities
input_fields.p8_pain_severity_ratings: right shoulder as a 6-7 out of 10, the pain in her lower back and left knee as a 7 out of 10, and the pain in her left hip as a 9 out of 10

few_shot_examples.output_text: Ms. Bachar states that from 01/01/2010 to 02/28/2023, she was involved in industrial injuries in the course of performing her usual and customary job duties. Ms. Bachar reports that as a result of performing repetitive work activities, she developed the gradual onset of pain in her right shoulder, lower back, left hip, and in her left knee. She rates the severity of pain in her right shoulder as a 6-7 out of 10, the pain in her lower back and left knee as a 7 out of 10, and the pain in her left hip as a 9 out of 10, all on a VAS. Ms. Bachar finds that in response to her industrial related orthopedic pain, she developed emotional stressors. Ms. Bachar finds she is clenching her teeth and bracing her facial musculature in response to her orthopedic pain, and also in response to the resultant emotional stressors experienced. This has caused her to develop facial pain. Ms. Bachar states she has gained approximately 10-15 lbs since her injury. Ms. Bachar is taking the medication of Ibuprofen, 800 mg 3 times per week, and Lexapro, 10 mg daily, on an industrial basis.

sections.title: Treatment Received for Industrial Injury
sections.generation_prompt: Generate a concise treatment narrative for industrial injury. Start with surgery details if available in the data, followed by other treatments received. Use {patient_title} {patient_last_name} and maintain a narrative tone. Include specific details about treatments like physical therapy, chiropractic manipulations, acupuncture, and psychological therapy. Do not invent information not present in the data. If 'p7_tx_received' is 'None', state that the patient has received no treatment. Use past tense and static phrases like 'has received treatments of' and 'underwent'. Ensure the narrative is generated only if relevant data is present.

few_shot_examples.output_text: Mr. Anderson underwent left ankle surgery in 2024. Mr. Anderson has received treatments of physical therapy.
Ms. Bachar has received treatments of physical therapy and chiropractic manipulations.
Mr. Canseco Gutierrez has received no treatment.

sections.title: Past Medical History
sections.generation_prompt: Generate a past medical history narrative for {patient_title} {patient_last_name}. Start with '{patient_title} {patient_last_name} reports having' and list relevant medical conditions. Use the exact text from 'p8_past_medical_history' if available. For other conditions marked 'Yes', mention them naturally without adding specific details not given. Include relevant details like 'He has undergone sleep study testing' for 'p1_breathing_problems' marked 'Yes'. Avoid hallucinations and redundant information. Do not invent conditions or details not present in the input data. If 'p8_past_medical_history' is available, use it as is. If no conditions are mentioned, state 'Past medical history is unremarkable.'

few_shot_examples.output_text: Ms. Bachar reports having stomach acids coming up into her mouth.
Mr. Rivera reports having high blood pressure, as well as stomach acids coming up into his mouth. He has undergone sleep study testing.

sections.title: Subjective Complaints
sections.generation_prompt:
Generate the SUBJECTIVE COMPLAINTS section as a BULLETED LIST organized into subsections.

STRUCTURE (use this exact order, SKIP any subsection where ALL its fields are null/empty):

1. **Headaches** (if p10_headache_frequency exists)
   Bullet format:
   - {frequency} {severity} headaches in the {locations}
   - Headache pain described as {quality}
   - Headaches occurring about {percent}% of the time
   - Headache pain intensity of {VAS} on a Visual Analog Scale of 0 to 10

2. **Facial Pain** (if p10_right_face_pain_frequency or p10_left_face_pain_frequency exists)
   Bullet format:
   - {frequency} {severity} facial pain on the right and left sides
   - Facial pain described as {quality}
   - Facial pain occurring about {percent}% of the time
   - Facial pain intensity of {VAS} on a Visual Analog Scale of 0 to 10
   - Facial pain aggravated by eating hard or chewy foods (if p12_eating_hard_chewy_food_pain is Yes)
   - Facial pain aggravated by prolonged continuous speaking up to {max_speak_time} minutes (if p12_prolonged_speaking_pain is Yes)
   - Facial pain interfering with ability to participate in social activities (if p12_social_activities_interference is Yes)
   - Facial pain interfering with ability to sleep (if p12_sleep_interference is Yes)
   - Facial pain interfering with ability to concentrate (if p12_concentration_interference is Yes)
   - Facial pain interfering in relationship with family/significant other (if p12_relationship_interference is Yes)
   - Facial pain causing irritability/anger (if p12_irritable_angry is Yes)
   - Facial pain causing increased anxiety and stress (always include if any facial pain interference/emotional fields are Yes)

3. **Temporomandibular Joint** (if p10_noises_in_tmj exists OR p10_right_tmj_pain_frequency exists)
   Opening: 'With regards to the TMJ, {patient_title} {patient_last_name} has complaints of:'
   Bullets:
   - Clicking noises in the {side} temporomandibular joints, which {he_she} first noticed about {years} prior to this examination
   - {frequency} {severity} pain in the {side} temporomandibular joint
   - TMJ pain occurring about {percent}% of the time with an intensity of {VAS} on a Visual Analog Scale of 0 to 10
   - Jaw locking in {position} positions on {count} occasions (if p11_jaw_locking exists)
   - Having to self-manipulate {his_her} jaw back into position

4. **Dental** (always include if p7_orthopedic_pain_clenching is Yes)
   Bullets:
   - Clenching and grinding {his_her} teeth and bracing {his_her} facial musculature in response to the industrial related orthopedic pain and resultant emotional stressors
   - Clenching and grinding {his_her} teeth during the day and at night during sleep
   - Facial pain upon smiling (if p11_facial_pain_smiling)
   - Facial pain upon yawning (if p11_facial_pain_yawning)
   - Soreness of teeth upon waking in the morning (if p11_soreness_teeth_waking is Yes)
   - Soreness of face/jaw upon waking in the morning (if p11_soreness_face_jaw_waking is Yes)
   - Headaches in temple areas upon waking in the morning (if p11_headaches_temple_waking is Yes)
   - Limited opening of the mouth when compared to before the injury (if p11_limited_opening_mouth is Yes)
   - Bite feels off (if p11_bite_feels_off is Yes)
   - Feeling of a dry mouth (if p3_dry_mouth is Yes/Sometimes)
   - Experiencing hoarseness (if p3_hoarseness is Yes/Sometimes)
   - Difficulties swallowing (if p3_difficulty_swallowing is Yes/Sometimes)
   - Feeling {his_her} mouth is dry when eating a meal (if p3_mouth_dry_eating is Yes/Sometimes)
   - Need to sip liquids to aid in swallowing dry food (if p3_sip_liquids_aid is Yes/Sometimes)
   - Bleeding of the gums (if p11_bleeding_gums is Yes)

5. **Sleep Disturbances** (if any sleep field is Yes)
   Bullets:
   - Snoring (if p10_snoring is Yes)
   - Gasping for air during sleep (if p10_gasps_air_night is Yes)
   - Waking with heart beating uncontrollably (if p10_palpitations_awakening is Yes)
   - Breathing stops during sleep (if p10_breathing_cessation is Yes)
   - Feeling fatigued during the day (if p10_fatigue is Yes)

6. **Other relating complaints are:** (if ear_problems or speech issues exist)
   - Loss of hearing in the {side} ears (if p11_ear_problems has hearing loss)
   - Pain/ringing/buzzing in the {side} ears

CRITICAL RULES:
- Each bullet is ONE line, starting with the fact
- Do NOT include any bullet where the source field is null/empty
- Do NOT invent data — if a field is missing, skip that bullet entirely
- Do NOT include subsection headers for empty subsections
- Use {patient_title} {patient_last_name} for patient references
- Use {his_her} and {he_she} for pronouns
- For 'Sometimes' values, treat as Yes (include the bullet)
- For 'NO' values, do NOT include that bullet

sections.title: Diagnostic Bite Force Testing
sections.hybrid_template:
The muscles of mastication were tested for their functional capacity measuring the maximum bite force and strength of these facial muscles. The measurements were objectively determined with the use of a gnathodynamometer. These measurements objectively determine the maximum bite force of the muscles of mastication used in chewing, comparing one side to the other. The muscles of mastication are innervated by the fifth cranial nerve, the Trigeminal Nerve. The Trigeminal Nerve has a motor function and a sensory function. The motor function of the Trigeminal Nerve controls the bite force of the muscles of mastication. The measurements obtained using a gnathodynamometer objectively demonstrate any unequal biting forces of the muscles bilaterally, and also correlate and confirm the applicant’s complaint of not being able to eat hard and chewy foods by objectively quantifying the amount of the applicant’s bite force.
The objective measurements confirm {patient_title} {patient_last_name}’s complaint of not being able to eat hard and chewy foods because {patient_title} {patient_last_name} could only apply the maximum bite force of {Diagnostic_Bite_Force_Analysis_Left} Newtons upon biting, when the normal amount of Newton forces to eat hard and chewy foods should exceed 400 Newtons. (400 Newtons = 90 Pounds Force)
The objective measurements also confirm that {patient_title} {patient_last_name} has a weaker force of {his_her} bite on one side of {his_her} muscles of mastication as compared to the other side. The closing force of {his_her} right side measured at {p16_right_newtons} Newtons, while closing force of the left side of {his_her} closing muscles of mastication was {p16_left_newtons} Newtons.
"Gnathodynamometer--a reliable device for assessing jaw bite force." Shushma, G., Kantly, R. Journal of Evolution of Medical and Dental Sciences, vol. 5, no. 102, 22 Dec. 2016, pp. 7488+

Section 4604.5(d) of the Labor Code states: "for all injuries not covered by the official utilization schedule adopted pursuant to Section 5307.27, authorized treatment shall be in accordance with other evidence-based medical treatment guidelines that are recognized generally by the national medical community and scientifically based."
The dental specialty of Orofacial Pain has been established by the American Dental Association, and its specialty association has published standard of care guidelines for the assessment, diagnosis, and management of Orofacial Pain in the following text:
Orofacial Pain Guidelines for Assessment, Diagnosis, and Management, Sixth Edition. Leeuw, Reny de, editor. | Klasser, Gary D., editor.  American Academy of Orofacial Pain.2018 Quintessence Publishing Co, Inc.
On page 170 it states:
"Conservative (ie. reversible), noninvasive treatment such as self-management instructions, behavioral modification, physical therapy, medications, and orthopedic appliances are endorsed for the initial care of nearly all TMDs."
Greene CS. Managing the care of patients with temporomandibular disorders: A new guideline for care. J Am Dent Assoc 2010; 141:1086–1088
On page 181 of the Sixth Edition of the Orofacial Pain Guidelines for Assessment, Diagnosis. and Management published by the American Academy of Orofacial Pain, it states:
"Orthopedic appliances, including interocclusal splints, orthotics, orthoses, bite guards, bite planes, night guards, or bruxism appliances, are routinely used in the treatment of TMDs. Based on current theory, removable acrylic resin appliances that cover the teeth have traditionally been used to alter occlusal relationships and to redistribute occlusal forces, to prevent bruxism and TMDs progression."

sections.generation_prompt: Generate the legal disclosure notice for a workers' compensation dental report. Use the provided patient information and examiner details. The section has a highly standardized structure with specific paragraphs and a doctor signature block. Use the patient's name and title in the first sentence. Include 'I used an interpreter, as noted herein.' if the 'p1_intp' field is 'true'. Do not invent or include any information not specified in the source fields or static phrases. Ensure that the generated text includes all required paragraphs and the doctor's signature block with all necessary details. Do not omit any of the standard paragraphs or the signature block details.

generation_notes:
These rules generate comprehensive clinical dental reports for workers' compensation cases. The report follows a standardized structure with both required universal sections and conditional sections based on clinical findings. Key features include:

1. Formatted header with claim information
2. Narrative sections using clinical tone and proper medical terminology  
3. Conditional sections that appear only when relevant findings are present
4. ADL table with difficulty severity levels
5. Comprehensive diagnostic testing results
6. ICD-10 diagnosis codes with clinical descriptions

The rules handle missing data gracefully and maintain consistent clinical language throughout. Special attention is given to bruxism/clenching as the primary industrial-related dental condition, with supporting diagnostic findings from various tests and examinations.
